action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 28's local test with the current piecewise controller still shows early termination (min_d 0.78-0.90) and only 83.7 avg safe steps. The pathfinder at dist>3.5 is too aggressive in forward speed, and the local controller doesn't decelerate early enough when approaching the human. Analysis: The key insight is that pathfinding to the human's exact position causes overshoot and early episode termination. Instead, I will pathfind to a target point that is 1.5m from the human toward the robot (i.e., the sweet spot). This makes the pathfinder naturally guide the robot to safe social distance rather than to the human itself. The local controller will be made extremely conservative with early deceleration (max 0.25 forward when within 2.2m, strong backup below 1.2m, neutral at 1.2-1.5m). The pathfinder will only activate when dist > 3.5 AND geodesic distance to the offset target exceeds 2.5m. Decision: Rewrite model.py with offset-target pathfinder + ultra-conservative local controller. Run train.py + local 3-episode test. Since we have 2 iterations remaining and ~7.3h, we will run the local test and immediately submit a 50-episode eval to get real harness feedback this iteration.
